title: Mental Disorders Act, 1949

sections.title: 2. Interpretation
7. Magistrate may order apprehension in certain cases
sections.body:
Amagistratehaving jurisdiction, if satisfied upon information on oath that a person is apparently mentally disordered or defective and is—
Amagistratehaving jurisdiction, if satisfied upon information on oath that a person is apparently mentally disordered or defective and is—
(a)dangerous to himself or to others; or
dangerous to himself or to others; or

sections.title: 8. Officer may apprehend without warrant in certain cases
9. Inquiry into state of mind ofpatient
sections.body:
Where amagistratehas instituted aninquiry, at any time prior to the completion of thatinquiryhe may order the person in whose charge the suspected person is to produce the suspected person at such time and place as may be set out in such order.
Where amagistratehas instituted aninquiry, at any time prior to the completion of thatinquiryhe may order the person in whose charge the suspected person is to produce the suspected person at such time and place as may be set out in such order.

sections.title: 10. Magistrate may interrogatepatientand must obtain certificate
sections.body: mentally normal; or

sections.title: 11. Adjudication order
sections.body: is not under proper care, treatment or control; or

sections.title: 12. Procedure when noadjudication ordermade
sections.body:
When aninquiryis held and noadjudication orderis made, themagistrateshall discharge thepatientand revoke any warrant of detention and so inform any person in whose care thepatientmay have been detained.
When aninquiryis held and noadjudication orderis made, themagistrateshall discharge thepatientand revoke any warrant of detention and so inform any person in whose care thepatientmay have been detained.

sections.title: 13. Control orders
sections.body:
After anadjudication orderhas been made, themagistrateshall make acontrol order, for the control, care or detention of thepatient, specifying either that thepatientbe—
After anadjudication orderhas been made, themagistrateshall make acontrol order, for the control, care or detention of thepatient, specifying either that thepatientbe—
(a)detained in a prescribed place; or
detained in a prescribed place; or
detained in a prescribed place; or
(b)handed over to the care and control of his friends or relatives, or to a chief or village headman.
handed over to the care and control of his friends or relatives, or to a chief or village headman.
handed over to the care and control of his friends or relatives, or to a chief or village headman.

sections.title: 14. Removal out of Zambia
sections.body:
Everypatientin respect of whom anadjudication orderand acontrol orderis in force may be removed to a specified place outside Zambia by a warrant signed by the*Minister.*Powers delegated to Director of Medical Services by S.I. No. 57 of 1964.
Everypatientin respect of whom anadjudication orderand acontrol orderis in force may be removed to a specified place outside Zambia by a warrant signed by the*Minister.
*Powers delegated to Director of Medical Services by S.I. No. 57 of 1964.

sections.title: 16. Patient to remain in place to which removed
sections.body:
Anypatientremoved by virtue of a warrant signed under section fourteen shall remain in the place to which he has been removed until the Minister shall otherwise direct, or until thepatient's release or discharge as in this section or, as the case may be, in section twenty-two is provided.
Anypatientremoved by virtue of a warrant signed under section fourteen shall remain in the place to which he has been removed until the Minister shall otherwise direct, or until thepatient's release or discharge as in this section or, as the case may be, in section twenty-two is provided.

sections.title: 17. HighCourtjurisdiction
sections.body:
There shall be vested in the HighCourtjurisdiction to administer and control the estates and property of patients, including the power to appoint committees and receivers, in substantial conformity with the law and practice for the time being in force in the HighCourtof Justice in England.
There shall be vested in the HighCourtjurisdiction to administer and control the estates and property of patients, including the power to appoint committees and receivers, in substantial conformity with the law and practice for the time being in force in the HighCourtof Justice in England.

sections.title: 19. Powers ofRegistrarand Administrator-General
sections.body:
For the purposes of this Act, in default of any prescribed rules, theRegistrarshall exercise all the powers and duties of the Master in Lunacy or of theCourtof Protection in England, and the Administrator-General shall exercise all the powers and duties of the Official Solicitor, with regard to the estates and property of patients.
For the purposes of this Act, in default of any prescribed rules, theRegistrarshall exercise all the powers and duties of the Master in Lunacy or of theCourtof Protection in England, and the Administrator-General shall exercise all the powers and duties of the Official Solicitor, with regard to the estates and property of patients.

sections.title: 20. Discharge on certificate of sanity
sections.body:
Where anadjudication orderhas been made and any two medical practitioners have each issued a certificate of sanity in the prescribed form, amagistrateshall grant thepatientan order of discharge in the prescribed form and furnish him with a certified copy thereof.
Where anadjudication orderhas been made and any two medical practitioners have each issued a certificate of sanity in the prescribed form, amagistrateshall grant thepatientan order of discharge in the prescribed form and furnish him with a certified copy thereof.

sections.title: 21. Conditional releasepermit
22. Re-entry into Zambia ofpatientremoved by warrant
sections.body:
Apatientwho has been removed from Zambia by virtue of a warrant issued under section fourteen shall, on re-entering Zambia, report within twenty-four hours to the District Secretary at the place of re entry into Zambia.
Apatientwho has been removed from Zambia by virtue of a warrant issued under section fourteen shall, on re-entering Zambia, report within twenty-four hours to the District Secretary at the place of re entry into Zambia.

sections.title: 23. Patients discharged in Southern Rhodesia
sections.body: adjudicated a lunatic under the provisions of the Lunacy Act, Chapter 28 of the 1950 Edition of the Laws;

sections.title: 24. Amendment of orders and certificates
sections.body:
If an application for an order or an order is found to be in any respect incorrect or deficient, themagistratewho made it, or his successor in office, or amagistratelawfully acting for him or for his successor, maypermitthe application to be amended, or may, as the case may be, amend the order.
If an application for an order or an order is found to be in any respect incorrect or deficient, themagistratewho made it, or his successor in office, or amagistratelawfully acting for him or for his successor, maypermitthe application to be amended, or may, as the case may be, amend the order.

sections.title: 25. False statements, entries and wilful obstruction
sections.body: makes any wilful misstatement of any material fact in any report, certificate, statement or document made in pursuance of this Act;

sections.title: 29. Penalties
sections.body:
Any person who commits an offence against this Act in respect of which no penalty is by this Act expressly provided shall be liable to a fine not exceeding six hundred penalty units or to imprisonment for a period not exceeding three months, or to both
Any person who commits an offence against this Act in respect of which no penalty is by this Act expressly provided shall be liable to a fine not exceeding six hundred penalty units or to imprisonment for a period not exceeding three months, or to both

sections.title: 32. Limitations of actions by patients
sections.body:
Where a person has done anything in pursuance or in intended pursuance of any of the provisions of this Act, he shall not be liable to any civil or criminal proceedings, whether on the ground of want or jurisdiction or on any other ground, unless he has acted in bad faith or without reasonable care.
Where a person has done anything in pursuance or in intended pursuance of any of the provisions of this Act, he shall not be liable to any civil or criminal proceedings, whether on the ground of want or jurisdiction or on any other ground, unless he has acted in bad faith or without reasonable care.

sections.title: 34. Place of admittance to and powers of hearing
sections.body:
Amagistrate, in his discretion, may hold aninquiryin a room or place other than that in which thesubordinate courtnormally sits.
Amagistrate, in his discretion, may hold aninquiryin a room or place other than that in which thesubordinate courtnormally sits.

sections.title: 35. Cost of maintenance of patients
sections.body:
When any person is detained under the provisions of this Act in any place, the maintenance and other costs and expenses of such person shall, until further provision therefor is made, be defrayed out of the general revenues of the Republic.
When any person is detained under the provisions of this Act in any place, the maintenance and other costs and expenses of such person shall, until further provision therefor is made, be defrayed out of the general revenues of the Republic.

sections.title: 39. Regulations
sections.body:
The Minister may, by statutory instrument, make regulations for the due administration and efficient working of this Act.
The Minister may, by statutory instrument, make regulations for the due administration and efficient working of this Act.
